note: forge-bridge connection to Flame 2026.2.1 is unavailable (no live Flame session during this execution). Per plan Task 1 blocker rule: documenting bridge-offline and proceeding with conservative default.

prior_findings.finding: flame.batch.frame_rate was observed to be a NoneType slot on Flame 2026.2.1 during Phase 2 probing. This is why D-14 fallback exists.

conservative_default.primary_description: Attempt flame.batch.frame_rate.get_value() with defensive try/except. On Flame 2026.2.1 this was a NoneType slot (D-19) but may have been patched; the try/except covers both cases.

label_mapping.strategy: Map the raw fps value to a _FLAME_FPS_LABELS label string. If the source returns a label string already (e.g. '24 fps'), pass through. If it returns a numeric (e.g. 24.0), map to the nearest label within 0.1 tolerance. If no match, stamp '<num> fps' and emit a stderr warning.

fallback_chain: 1. flame.batch.frame_rate.get_value() — may be NoneType slot on 2026.2.1
2. '24 fps' + stderr warning (ABSOLUTE LAST RESORT; never silent)
conservative_default.rationale: flame.project.current_project.frame_rate could not be verified without a live Flame session. flame.batch.frame_rate is the closest documented path. D-14 fallback ensures the stamp always happens with a loud warning, never silently.
